title: Rule-Conditioned Dry vs Wet Strategy State Features
summary: Official regulations and tyre docs imply structurally different pit logic in dry versus wet races: dry races require two dry compounds unless intermediates/wets are used, while wet conditions change both legality pressure and degradation/warm-up behavior.
rationale: Official regulations and tyre docs imply structurally different pit logic in dry versus wet races: dry races require two dry compounds unless intermediates/wets are used, while wet conditions change both legality pressure and degradation/warm-up behavior. The current best features flag wet weather but do not appear to model the rule/state transition explicitly.
implementation_hint: Split strategy state into dry-legal and wet-exempt regimes. In dry conditions, add features for 'mandatory second dry compound still needed', 'currently on first/second unique slick', and 'laps remaining after hypothetical stop with a different slick'. In wet conditions, instead emphasize warm-up/degradation asymmetry and downweight dry-compound completion logic. Test a regime-specialized blend or simple interaction features rather than one global heuristic.
expected_effect: Reduces noise from applying dry-race tyre-completion logic to wet/intermediate scenarios and sharpens end-of-race stop pressure in dry races.
risk: The dataset may not expose full compound-allocation legality, so the rule proxy can be approximate; wet samples may be sparse.
